@graph.name: TouchDesigner Signal Flow Ontology
@graph.description: A conceptual model describing node-based visual programming systems used in TouchDesigner, including operators, signals, parameters, and network structure.

@graph.name: Node
@graph.description: A node is a functional unit within a visual programming graph. Each node represents an operator that receives input signals, performs a transformation, and produces output signals.

@graph.name: Operator
@graph.description: An operator is a computational component in TouchDesigner that processes signals. Operators are instantiated as nodes inside a network and are categorized by operator family.

@graph.name: Signal
@graph.description: A signal is a stream of data moving between operators in a TouchDesigner network. Signals may represent textures, numeric channels, geometry structures, data tables, particle states, or rendering materials.

@graph.name: Parameter
@graph.description: A parameter is a configurable property of an operator controlling how the operator processes incoming signals and produces output signals.

@graph.name: Network
@graph.description: A network is a directed graph of operators connected by signal paths. Networks define the flow of data through a TouchDesigner patch.

@graph.name: Patch
@graph.description: A patch is the complete TouchDesigner project consisting of one or more interconnected operator networks that collectively define a real-time system.

@graph.name: Operator Family
@graph.description: An operator family is a classification grouping operators by the type of signal they process.

@graph.name: TOP
@graph.description: Texture Operators process image and pixel data. TOP networks perform compositing, filtering, rendering, and other GPU-based image operations.

@graph.name: CHOP
@graph.description: Channel Operators process time-based numeric signals sampled over frames. CHOPs are commonly used for animation control, motion signals, and data modulation.

@graph.name: SOP
@graph.description: Surface Operators process geometric structures composed of points, vertices, primitives, and attributes.

@graph.name: DAT
@graph.description: Data Operators manage structured information such as tables, text, and scripts used for control logic and metadata.

@graph.name: POP
@graph.description: Particle Operators simulate particle systems including emission, forces, and particle attribute evolution.

@graph.name: MAT
@graph.description: Material Operators define shading properties used during rendering, including surface color, lighting interaction, and texture mapping.
